# Daily Report — 2026-04-21

## Task Completed
Added pagination helpers for list operations. Three new functions in `helpers.py`:

- `_build_sysparm_params(limit, offset, query, display_value, exclude_reference_link, order_by, fields)` — builds the standard `sysparm_*` query-parameter dict for Table API list requests, eliminating the repeated 4-line boilerplate in every tool.
- `_join_query_parts(parts)` — joins filter strings with the ServiceNow `^` operator, skipping empty entries so callers can append conditions unconditionally.
- `_paginated_list_response(items, limit, offset, result_key, extra)` — builds a standardised success response that adds `has_more` (bool) and `next_offset` (int | None) so callers can page through results without needing a separate count query.

Applied across four tool modules:
- `incident_tools.py` — `list_incidents` refactored to use all three helpers.
- `syslog_tools.py` — `list_syslog_entries` refactored to use all three helpers.
- `knowledge_base.py` — `list_knowledge_bases`, `list_articles`, `list_categories` success responses migrated to `_paginated_list_response`.

All three helpers are exported from `utils/__init__.py`.

## Test Results
- Total tests: 564 passed, 4 deselected
- Coverage: 89% overall; helpers.py 81% (new helpers are exercised by unit tests)
- All passing: yes

## Security
Project code clean. Pre-installed system packages (pip, setuptools, pyjwt, wheel) have known CVEs unrelated to this project.

## Files Changed
- `src/servicenow_mcp/utils/helpers.py` — added `_build_sysparm_params`, `_join_query_parts`, `_paginated_list_response`
- `src/servicenow_mcp/utils/__init__.py` — exported three new helpers
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/incident_tools.py` — `list_incidents` uses new helpers
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/syslog_tools.py` — `list_syslog_entries` uses new helpers
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/knowledge_base.py` — list functions use `_paginated_list_response`
- `tests/test_pagination_helpers.py` — 46 new unit tests covering all three helpers
